title: Senolytic field shifts from elimination to precision: deleterious subpopulation clearance now the goal
description: Multiple May reviews and Fight Aging coverage converge on a new senolytic paradigm: instead of broadly killing senescent cells, the next generation aims to clear deleterious subpopulations while preserving beneficial ones, paired with upstream prevention. This refines what counts as a viable geroprotector and reshapes the Mayo/Unity/Cellage pipelines.

title: Diet rewires biological age in just 4 weeks: Sydney study published in Aging Cell
description: University of Sydney researchers showed adults aged 65-75 reduced biological age markers in 4 weeks on lower-fat / higher-carb diets, while business-as-usual diets showed no change. The result is the fastest published dietary bio-age signal to date and a tractable lifestyle protocol for clinicians.

title: BCLXL-PROTAC senolytic emerges as lead candidate for COPD lung rejuvenation
description: Proteolysis-targeting chimera senolytics that degrade BCL-xL show potent, selective clearance of senescent lung cells in preclinical studies, positioning COPD as the first inhaled senolytic indication. Following ERS Phase preclinical data and Nature Aging's 753b paper, the modality moves from concept to lead.

title: AI drug discovery captures longevity capital: Lilly-Insilico $2.75B deal anchors trend
description: The recently disclosed Eli Lilly / Insilico Medicine collaboration valued at up to $2.75B underscores how AI-discovered geroprotectors are now the preferred deployment for big-pharma longevity capital. Gero-Chugai and Biophytis at NVIDIA GTC add momentum to the AI-driven pipeline thesis.

title: Pre-symptomatic dementia detection wins capital: Prema Cognition closes oversubscribed $730K seed
description: Cambridge-derived Prema Cognition raised $730K (oversubscribed) for PREMAZ, a memory-precision test that flags cognitive decline years earlier than conventional tools. Investor demand signals capital rotation into preventive brain-health diagnostics ahead of next-gen Alzheimer therapeutics.

title: Prema Cognition secures backing for earlier dementia detection
description: Cambridge-derived startup raises $730k (£550k) led by SFC Capital to expand PREMAZ, a digital cognitive assessment designed to spot memory decline years earlier than conventional tests.

title: Diathrive partners with AliveCor to target heart-diabetes risk
description: Diathrive Health and AliveCor's KardiaComplete launch an integrated cardiometabolic platform to combine diabetes management with cardiovascular monitoring for employer health programs.

title: Aging, Senolytics, and Cost-Benefit Evaluation
description: Fight Aging argues senolytic therapies already exist and now require rigorous cost-benefit evaluation across specific age-related contexts rather than further proof-of-concept.

title: Cellular senescence: from pathogenic mechanisms to precision anti-aging interventions
description: Review piece argues senescence research is shifting from biomarker-based identification toward precision clearance of deleterious senescent subpopulations and upstream prevention.

title: Platelet HuR/ELAVL1 deletion reduces inflammation and senescence in aged mice
description: Researchers identify HuR/ELAVL1 as a regulator of inflammatory platelet behavior in aging; platelet-specific HuR deletion reduces inflammation, senescence burden, and age-related functional decline.

title: LPS priming accelerates wound healing in aged mice
description: A short pulse of bacterial lipopolysaccharide before wounding markedly accelerates skin repair in aged mice by priming immune-cell behavior and improving epithelial barrier restoration.

title: Melanoma age-dependence in mice: γδ T cells and PROS1 implicated
description: Melanoma spread is lowest in very young and very old mice and peaks in middle age — γδ T cells and PROS1 explain the unexpected age-dependent pattern, with implications for using older animal models.

title: Physical activity lowers epigenetic age acceleration: meta-analysis
description: Systematic review and meta-analysis finds higher physical activity is consistently associated with lower Horvath and GrimAge epigenetic age acceleration, though most evidence is cross-sectional.

title: Group-living mammals live longer: 1,436-species analysis
description: Across 1,436 mammal species, group-living and pair-living species had longer lifespans than solitary species after controlling for body mass and phylogeny — adding evidence for social structure as a longevity driver.

title: Adult Integrator depletion extends C. elegans lifespan and healthspan
description: Loss of most Integrator complex subunits in adult C. elegans extends lifespan and healthspan via disrupted RNA 3′ processing, altered siRNA levels, and mitochondrial gene expression changes.

title: Celastrol mimics PGC-1α/exercise signature and improves sarcopenia in mice
description: Celastrol was identified as a candidate that mimics PGC-1α overexpression or exercise; it improved muscle differentiation and mitochondrial function in mice and extended C. elegans lifespan.

title: Photobiomodulation increases neuromuscular synapse activity in aged rats
description: Low-level red light treatment increased ultrastructural signs of neuromuscular synapse activity in aged rats, suggesting compensatory reorganization of aged neuromuscular junctions.

title: Bacterial outer-membrane vesicles linked to Alzheimer's
description: Review proposes that bacterial outer membrane vesicles from oral and gut microbes may help link peripheral microbial reservoirs to neuroimmune and metabolic dysfunction in Alzheimer's disease.

title: Scientists reversed biological age in older adults with a 4-week diet change
description: University of Sydney trial in adults 65-75 found 4 weeks of lower-fat or more plant-based protein diets reduced estimated biological age; strongest signal from the lower-fat, higher-carb arm.

title: Diathrive Health and KardiaComplete announce strategic partnership
description: AliveCor's KardiaComplete and Diathrive Health formalize a partnership to bundle diabetes and cardiovascular monitoring for employer cardiometabolic-risk programs.

title: Prema Cognition raises £550k startup funding
description: Pre-seed round led by SFC Capital with angel syndicate from pharma, biotech, enterprise software and longevity backers; funds clinical dataset expansion and regulatory pathway for PREMAZ.

title: Endlyz raises Seed 2 financing, leans into root-cause neuroscience
description: London-based Endlyz Therapeutics closes Seed 2 backed by AbbVie, Dementia Discovery Fund, Oxford Science Enterprises and Parkinson's UK to advance ATP13A2/ATP10B PAM programs for Parkinson's disease.

title: Targeting Senescent Cells as a Treatment for COPD
description: Review of BCLXL-PROTAC senolytics showing potent and selective clearance of senescent lung cells in preclinical models — a potential novel therapy for COPD and other age-related lung diseases.

title: Infinite Epigenetics acquires Tally Health to scale epigenetic-driven longevity
description: Infinite Epigenetics acquires Tally Health and its cheek-swab biological age test and supplement protocols, building one of the largest epigenetic datasets to advance personalized longevity medicine.

title: Longevity Week Roundup — Week 18, 2026
description: Roundup covers Infinite Epigenetics / Tally Health, SuperAge Games in NYC, Endlyz Seed 2, Genflow-Acuitas SIRT6 mRNA partnership, and Smith+Nephew AGILI-C 5-year knee data.

title: AI drug discovery is reshaping longevity medicine
description: Eli Lilly's $2.75B commitment to Insilico Medicine, Gero-Chugai partnership and Biophytis NVIDIA GTC platform demonstrate that AI-discovered geroprotectors are now the preferred deployment for big-pharma longevity capital.

title: Insilico-Lilly $2.75B AI drug discovery deal
description: Eli Lilly enters $2.75B collaboration with Insilico Medicine for AI-discovered drug candidates, including $115M upfront and milestones plus royalties; 28 Insilico molecules in pipeline.

title: Large rapamycin clinical trial launches at UT Health San Antonio
description: NIA-funded multi-phase study of rapamycin and everolimus in healthy adults 65-90 launches at UT San Antonio, structured to define optimal anti-aging dose ahead of a 6-month randomized placebo-controlled trial in 84 participants.

title: ARPA-H awards $144M to 7 PROSPR teams for healthspan trials
description: ARPA-H allocates up to $144M across seven academic and biotech teams to lay the groundwork for Phase 3 trials of healthspan interventions, including rapamycin, SGLT-2 inhibitors and semaglutide in healthy 60-year-olds.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup April 2026: SRN-901, BCLXL-PROTAC, Tomatidine
description: April roundup highlights SRN-901 extending median remaining lifespan 33% in 18-month-old mice, BCLXL-PROTAC for COPD, and Tomatidine as a senotherapeutic improving cognition in aged mice.
